message:
feat(native_events+runtime_compat): tick 168 end-to-end cx chain through native event dispatch (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Nineteenth post-storage tick. Closes the orphan-cx hole that tick
165's lesson predicted would exist somewhere in the tree: the
native event dispatch path.

Problem: `run_with_cx` (line 270) spawned per-connection tasks via
`handle_connection` (legacy), which called `dispatch_event` →
`dispatch_event_with_timeout`. The leaf used
`crate::cx::for_request()` for the `event_tx.reserve(cx)` wait,
orphaning the cancellation chain. A caller-cancelled
`run_with_cx` would keep dispatching queued events until their
individual EVENT_SEND_TIMEOUT elapsed.

New siblings in native_events.rs:
- handle_connection_with_cx — cx-threaded line-read (tick 160's
  `next_line_with_cx` now reachable on both asupersync and
  non-asupersync paths, see below) + cx-threaded dispatch.
- dispatch_event_with_cx — thin delegate to
  `dispatch_event_with_timeout_with_cx`.
- dispatch_event_with_timeout_with_cx — routes the
  `event_tx.reserve(cx)` wait through `timeout_with_cx(cx, ...)`
  so cancellation unblocks the reserve immediately rather than
  waiting the full send_timeout. Err surfaces as Backpressure
  (matches legacy — "couldn't deliver, drop").

Call-site migration in `run_with_cx` (L270):
- L301 spawn: `handle_connection(stream, tx)` →
  `handle_connection_with_cx(child_cx, stream, tx)` with
  `let child_cx = cx.clone();` added. Each per-connection task
  carries its own cx clone.

Legacy `run` at L216 preserved unchanged.

Prerequisite fix in runtime_compat.rs:
- Tick 160 landed `unix::next_line_with_cx` only in the
  `#[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]` unix module.
  This tick adds the mirror to the asupersync variant. Oversight
  caught by the native_events migration.

Verified via fork bypass: 78/78 native_events:: tests pass,
including listener_emits_events + listener_accepts_reconnect_
after_disconnect which transitively cover the new dispatch chain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
